articles.title: The failures of prediction markets are predictable
articles.summary: Prediction markets consistently fail in ways that follow clear, identifiable patterns rather than random chance. These systematic blind spots create profitable opportunities for sharp bettors who understand where crowds reliably get it wrong.

articles.title: 'Informed' traders on Polymarket netted $143 million in 'anomalous' profit since 2024, researchers find
articles.summary: A new study found that sophisticated traders on Polymarket made $143 million in unusual profits throughout 2024, suggesting they had access to better information or trading strategies than typical users. This confirms what many suspected about prediction markets: the house edge isn't just the platform fee, it's also getting picked off by traders who know something you don't.

articles.title: Coinbase’s Top Lawyer Says Prediction Markets Aren’t Gambling—and Predicts Supreme Court Will Agree
articles.summary: Coinbase's chief legal officer argued that prediction markets are fundamentally different from gambling and expects the Supreme Court to eventually rule in favor of this distinction. If the high court agrees, it could demolish state-level restrictions on prediction markets and unleash a wave of legal betting on everything from elections to economic indicators.

articles.title: Kalshi ‘prediction market’ violates Washington's antigambling laws, AG says
articles.summary: Washington's Attorney General claims Kalshi violates state gambling laws, marking another regulatory challenge for the CFTC-approved prediction market platform. This signals that state-level enforcement could fragment the legal landscape for prediction markets, even when they have federal approval.

articles.title: Kalshi Approved for Margin Trading as It Lures Wall Street Pros
articles.summary: Kalshi just got regulatory approval to offer margin trading, letting traders leverage their bets on everything from elections to economic indicators. This puts them in direct competition with traditional Wall Street derivatives markets and signals that prediction markets are finally being taken seriously by institutional money.

articles.title: WA sues 'just a bookie with a fancy name'
articles.summary: Washington state is suing a betting operator, dismissing it as nothing more than a traditional bookie disguised with modern branding. The case could set precedent for how regulators classify and prosecute prediction market platforms that blur the line between sophisticated forecasting and old-school gambling.

articles.title: Dan Patrick adds data centers, prediction markets and THC to Senate priorities
articles.summary: Texas Lieutenant Governor Dan Patrick announced his legislative priorities for the upcoming session, which surprisingly include data centers, prediction markets, and THC alongside his usual conservative agenda. This signals that prediction markets could gain legitimate regulatory footing in one of America's largest states, potentially opening the floodgates for broader adoption across red states that typically follow Texas's lead.

articles.title: Insider trading concerns around the Iran war are on the rise. Can anyone police the bets?
articles.summary: Traders are increasingly betting on oil prices and defense stocks ahead of major geopolitical events, raising red flags about potential insider information from government and military sources. Prediction markets face the same vulnerability since oil futures and conflict odds move on the same leaked intel that corrupts traditional securities markets.

articles.title: NYSE-parent Intercontinental Exchange invests $600 million in Polymarket
articles.summary: NYSE-parent Intercontinental Exchange just dropped $600M into Polymarket, the crypto-based prediction market that's been dominating election betting. This signals Wall Street's serious bet that prediction markets are moving from crypto curiosity to mainstream financial infrastructure.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Scaled to USD 21B in Monthly Volume in 2026 | TRM Blog
articles.summary: Prediction markets hit $21B in monthly trading volume by 2026, marking explosive growth from today's relatively niche market. This scale puts prediction markets in the same league as major asset classes and signals they're finally breaking into mainstream finance where real money follows real insights.

articles.title: Gavin Newsom to ban California officials from insider betting on prediction markets
articles.summary: California Governor Gavin Newsom is moving to prohibit state officials from trading on prediction markets where they could leverage inside information. The ban signals growing mainstream legitimacy for prediction markets while creating clearer regulatory boundaries that could actually help the industry avoid bigger crackdowns.

articles.title: Homophones, Plurals and Language ‘Rulescucks’ Are Roiling Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Prediction markets are getting tangled up in semantic disputes over word interpretation, with traders arguing about whether "homophones" count as separate answers and how plurals should be resolved. These linguistic loopholes are creating profitable arbitrage opportunities for grammar nerds while frustrating casual bettors who thought they were betting on substance, not English class technicalities.
